answers: Pair programming: two developers, one computer. Driver writes code, Navigator reviews and thinks strategically. Switch roles often.
Benefits: fewer bugs, shared knowledge, faster onboarding, better design decisions, and more enjoyable coding.
Tips: switch roles every 15-30 minutes, communicate actively, take breaks, and be patient with different skill levels.
Pair programming works best for: complex problems, learning new codebases, mentoring juniors, and critical code.
